summary:
Adam and Krista reviewed the status of the Diligent website migration QA process. Adam had gone through the QA spreadsheet checking resource pages and reassigning items back to the Diligent team. They discussed how to handle the large volume of QA feedback (68+ unassigned items) - prioritizing actual bugs and broken functionality over cosmetic issues and scope creep like design changes or responsive padding tweaks.

Adam emphasized wanting to focus on high-priority pages like Elevate, virtual summit pages, and forms rather than getting bogged down with minor styling issues. They agreed on a workflow where minor notes get flagged as 'LR note' for later follow-up, while blatant bugs get immediate attention. Krista planned to block her calendar for content entry work the rest of the week. They also discussed Cameron's availability concerns - he was supposed to be working on Lively cookie implementation and IAS document store but had been partially MIA, and Surendra had been blocked for over a week needing debugging help.
keyDiscussionPoints: QA spreadsheet review - 68+ items unassigned to Last Rev team
Prioritization: focus on Elevate pages, virtual summits, forms over cosmetic fixes
Diligent team making design change requests during QA instead of just bug reports
Need to instruct Diligent to specify desktop vs mobile for responsive issues
Cameron MIA - not checking ticket queue, need to address availability
Surendra blocked for over a week - needs debugging help from Camilo
Content entry work needs to be collaborative with Diligent team
Header/footer Tailwind conversion needed for Sanity management
decisions: Minor cosmetic issues flagged as 'LR note' for post-launch follow-up
Blatant bugs and broken functionality get immediate attention
Design change requests pushed back to Diligent for post-launch
Krista to focus on content entry rest of the week
Need Cameron on backlog grooming call to address engagement

actionItems.action: Focus on Elevate pages and virtual summit implementation
Block calendar for content entry work
Talk to Cameron about availability and get him debugging Surendra's issue
Get Camilo to help Surendra get unblocked
Instruct Diligent to provide more detail in QA feedback (desktop/mobile)

followUp: Cameron's progress on Lively cookies and IAS document store
Diligent QA response and timeline expectations
